@graph.rdfs:label: any plant
@graph.rdfs:comment: Plants are the eukaryotic organisms that constitute the kingdom Plantae.

@graph.rdfs:label: grain
@graph.rdfs:comment: Poaceae, also called Gramineae, is a large and nearly ubiquitous family of monocotyledonous flowering plants commonly known as true grasses.

@graph.rdfs:label: wheat
@graph.rdfs:comment: Wheat is a group of wild and domesticated grasses of the genus Triticum.

@graph.rdfs:label: barley
@graph.rdfs:comment: Barley, a member of the grass family, is a major cereal grain grown in temperate climates globally.

@graph.rdfs:label: tomato
@graph.rdfs:comment: The tomato is a plant whose fruit is an edible berry that is eaten as a vegetable.

@graph.rdfs:label: cannabis
@graph.rdfs:comment: Cannabis sativa is an annual herbaceous flowering plant.

@graph.rdfs:label: plant height method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Height of the plant recorded with tape measure

@graph.rdfs:comment: Average branch length recorded with tape measure

@graph.rdfs:comment: Canopy diameter recorded with tape measure

@graph.rdfs:comment: Steam diameter recorded with calipers

@graph.rdfs:comment: Internode length with tape measure

@graph.rdfs:label: internode count method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Physical count using cotyledon node as 0, then counting subsequent nodes up the mainstem to the tip

@graph.rdfs:label: shoot primary branch count method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Physical count of branch number along the primary shoot axis, counting all nodes along the main stem from the base towards its tip

@graph.rdfs:label: shoot secondary branch count method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Physical count of secondary branch number (or count of secondary branches) using intersect of the oldest (or lowest) branch with main stem as 0, then counting subsequent nodes along that branch towards its tip

@graph.rdfs:label: leaf number method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Physical count of average number of leaf on a number of representative branches

@graph.rdfs:label: flower number trait method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Physical count of all flowers on a plant

@graph.rdfs:label: ripe fruit count method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Physical count of all ripe fruit on a plant

@graph.rdfs:label: unripe fruit count method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Physical count of all unripe / green fruit on a plant

@graph.rdfs:label: harvest weight method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Weighing plant material immediately during harvest (fresh weight) or after oven drying to a specified residual moisture content (dry weight)

@graph.rdfs:label: root density method
@graph.rdfs:comment: Visual assessment

@graph.rdfs:comment: Laboratory colorimetric assays
